title: The Harvest is Great
subtitle: Christ, moved with pity for the shepherdless crowds, sends out the Twelve

stillness: Be still, and let the crowds and the noise fall away.
Do not yet ask the Lord with your lips only.
Let your heart first be stirred to charity, that your asking may have merit.
For though God means to send, He wills to be entreated.
gospel: When Jesus saw the multitudes, He had compassion on them,
for they were harassed and lying down,
Then He said to His disciples:
The harvest indeed is great, but the laborers are few.
Pray therefore the Lord of the harvest,
that He send laborers into His harvest.
And calling His twelve Apostles, He gave them power over unclean spirits,
to cast them out, and to heal every disease and every infirmity.
And He sent them, two by two, to preach the kingdom of God,
Go not into the way of the Gentiles, He charged them,
and into the cities of the Samaritans enter not.
behold: Behold the multitudes gathered to Jesus,
lying abjectly upon the bare earth, under the open sky.
See them oppressed by labor of body and of soul,
by the burden of their bodies and the burden of their sins.
See them like sheep that have no shepherd to guide them.
And behold Jesus, looking upon them with the eye of mercy,
opening the bowels of His compassion toward them,
moved with pity, suffering together with them.
See the good Shepherd who feeds them without ceasing:
spiritually, by the word of His teaching,
and at times bodily also, by the help of earthly bread.
consider: The harassing of the flock is most often the fault of the shepherds.
As Jerome says: the affliction of the flock and the sheep is the guilt of the shepherds and the vice of the masters.
For the priests and teachers of that time, bent upon greed,
gave no rightful labor to their teaching,
but were as ravening wolves,
stripping the people of their earthly goods,
and by evil example calling them back from the things of the spirit.
And so it is today also — alas for the sorrow.
Many who are called shepherds do not fear to do the deeds of wolves.
They send out collectors of tithes and seizers of temporal things,
not inquisitors of heresy nor menders of vice.
Laborers there are who seek work, not idleness;
the burden, not the honor; souls, not tithes;
to be of profit, not to preside.
But few are the laborers, and many the hirelings;
few who seek the salvation of souls,
and many who chase the gain of passing things.
Therefore the Lord of the harvest must be entreated,
that He multiply the laborers and send them forth —
for though there are those who would hear good things,
there are wanting those who would speak.
He sent them two by two, as a sign of the charity required in preachers,
and that they might have the mutual comfort of fellowship,
for they were as yet weak.
Benign and merciful, the Lord does not begrudge His own powers to His servants;
yet great is the distance between having and bestowing, between giving and receiving.
Whatever He does, He does by His own power;
they, if they do anything, confess their own weakness and the power of the Lord,
saying: In the name of Jesus, rise and walk.
prayer: who, sending Your disciples out to preach,
gave them power to heal, and precepts for living and conversing:
direct my feet into the way of peace,
and give me health of mind and of body;
that I may have justice in my works,
and in all things Your fear and Your love.
and restore me, within and without, by Your gifts,
that the things You commanded Your disciples
I may, after my small measure, be worthy to imitate,
and with them, by Your mercy, be rewarded with eternal glory.

latinJewel.latin: Operarii sunt in Ecclesia qui quærunt opus, non otium; onus, non honorem; animas, non decimas; prodesse, non præesse.
latinJewel.english: The laborers in the Church are those who seek work, not idleness; the burden, not the honor; souls, not tithes; to be of profit, not to preside.
